judgments.catchwords: Administrative Law — Judicial review — Leave — Low threshold — Court acting as filter — Whether application frivolous, vexatious, hopeless or unarguable — Genuine arguable case warranting substantive hearing Administrative Law — Judicial review — Certiorari — Award of Strata Management Tribunal — Tribunal exercising statutory adjudicative and public functions — Award amenable to supervisory jurisdiction of High Court Administrative Law — Judicial review — Finality clause — Strata Management Act 2013, s 120 — Award final and binding and deemed court order — Finality not immunising unlawful decision from judicial review Constitutional Law — Judicial power — Supervisory jurisdiction of High Court — Federal Constitution, art 121 — Statutory tribunal remaining subject to legal limits Civil Procedure — Judicial review — Locus standi — Applicant directly liable under tribunal award — Person adversely affected under O 53 r 2(4) of the Rules of Court 2012 Civil Procedure — Judicial review — Time limit — Application filed approximately five weeks after award — Application made promptly and within three-month period under O 53 r 3(6) Civil Procedure — Judicial review — Procedural requirements — Statement, verifying affidavit and notice to Attorney General’s Chambers — O 53 rr 3(2) and 3(3) complied with Administrative Law — Reasons for decision — Strata Management Tribunal under express statutory duty to give reasons — Strata Management Act 2013, s 117(2) — Award containing operative order without findings or reasons — Arguable breach Administrative Law — Procedural fairness — Alleged failure to consider defence, counterclaim and written submissions — Fundamental issues concerning vacant possession and usability of parcel — Arguable denial of fair hearing Administrative Law — Relevant considerations — Tribunal award giving no indication that material matters were addressed — Whether failure affected validity of award — Issue requiring full record and inter partes hearing Strata Management — Maintenance charges — Commercial parcel allegedly incapable of occupation or use — Whether liability arises independently of effective delivery of vacant possession — Arguable question of statutory construction Administrative Law — Alternative remedy — Statutory procedures under ss 118 and 121 of the Strata Management Act 2013 — Mere existence of alternative avenue not rendering judicial review unarguable at leave stage Administrative Law — Judicial review — Supervisory rather than appellate jurisdiction — Grant of leave expressing no view on merits of intended challenge Civil Procedure — Stay of execution — Tribunal award enforceable as court order — Joint management body not heard at ex parte stage — Stay application adjourned for inter partes determination
judgments.judge: YA Dato' Sri Raja Segaran A/L S.Krishnan

judgments.catchwords: Land Law — Strata title — Purchasers having fully paid purchase price — Strata title remaining registered in developer’s name — Developer defunct and untraceable — Application to perfect registration Land Law — Sale and purchase — Beneficial ownership — Vendor becoming bare trustee upon completion — Full payment and purchaser’s entitlement to valid, complete and registrable transfer Trusts — Bare trust — Completed sale of strata parcel — Developer retaining only bare legal title — Bare trust not arising merely upon execution of sale and purchase agreement Equity — Vendor and purchaser — Equity regarding as done that which ought to be done — Ministerial failure of registration not defeating perfected beneficial interest Evidence — Documentary evidence — Deed of receipt and reassignment — Developer’s contemporaneous endorsement of consent — Proof that purchase price and financing had been fully discharged Equity — Laches — Long delay in perfecting transfer — Developer disappearing and administrative machinery unavailable — Bare trust constituting continuing trust — Delay fully explained Land Law — Registration of title — Court’s power to direct Registrar of Titles or Land Administrator to give effect to judgment — National Land Code 1965, ss 417 and 420 Land Law — Issue document of title — Original document held by untraceable developer or dissolved solicitors — Court directing issuance of new or replacement document and cancellation of prior issue document Civil Procedure — Execution of instruments — Developer unavailable to execute transfer documents — Deputy Registrar or Senior Assistant Registrar empowered to execute documents on developer’s behalf Civil Procedure — Declaratory relief — Beneficial ownership of identified strata parcel — Specific Relief Act 1950 — Consequential orders required to make declaration effective Civil Procedure — Substituted service — Defunct and untraceable company — Cumulative modes of service duly completed — Defendant deemed to have notice and electing not to participate Land Law — Torrens system — Company-register charges distinguished from interests registered against strata title — Official land search disclosing no charge, caveat or restriction Land Law — Registration — Safeguards for third parties — Orders operating without prejudice to interests registered at presentation — Service upon land authorities — Liberty to affected persons to apply

judgments.title: Pendakwa Raya v SYED SADDIQ BIN SYED ABDUL RAHMAN

parties.label: RESPONDEN
names: SYED SADDIQ BIN SYED ABDUL RAHMAN

judgments.catchwords: Abetting an offence of criminal breach of trust- disposal of the RM1 million was in violation of Article 24.8 and Art. 24.7 of the Bersatu Constitution- ordinary construction of the wording - “expenses” and not “withdrawal”- RM1 million withdrawn by PW13 was never misappropriated but was legitimately used for Armada’s programs Criminal misappropriation- property that is the subject of the alleged offence must belong to a person other than the Respondent- no evidence that the monies belonged to either ABBE or Armada- as the Respondent had incurred expenses in campaigning-reimbursement from the funds which came into the account which were from donors contributing to the cause of his campaigning.
judgments.judge: YA Dato' Collin Lawrence Sequerah
coram: YAA Datuk Seri Abu Bakar Bin Jais
YA Dato' Che Mohd Ruzima Bin Ghazali
YA Dato' Collin Lawrence Sequerah

docs.preparedBy: YA Dato' Collin Lawrence Sequerah

judgments.title: MUHAMMAD HAFIZ DANIEL BIN ABDULLAH v Pendakwa Raya & ors
judgments.catchwords: RAYUAN JENAYAH NO: W-05(M)-579-12/2022 & W-05(SH)-580-12/2022 MUHAMMAD HAFIZ DANIEL BIN ABDULLAH v PP Prosedur Jenayah — Rayuan terhadap sabitan dan hukuman — Tertuduh telah didakwa dengan tiga pertuduhan mengedar dadah berbahaya di bawah seksyen 39B(1)(a) Akta Dadah Berbahaya 1952 (ADB) dan satu pertuduhan bawah seksyen 30(3)/(5) Akta Racun 1952. Keterangan - samada kelewatan kira-kira tiga (3) tahun oleh Hakim Mahkamah Tinggi (HMT) dalam menyediakan alasan penghakiman ditambah dengan salah arah yang serius telah menjejaskan integriti penghakiman dan telah menyebabkan prejudis kepada perayu. Samada HMT telah melakukan kesilapan dalam keputusannya kerana gagal menilai terdapat kepincangan dalam keterangan yang dikemukakan yang menjadi intipati pertuduhan.
judgments.judge: YA Datuk Hayatul Akmal binti Abdul Aziz
coram: YA Dato' Paduka Azman Bin Abdullah
YA Datuk Hayatul Akmal binti Abdul Aziz
YA Datuk Meor Hashimi bin Abdul Hamid

docs.preparedBy: YA Datuk Hayatul Akmal binti Abdul Aziz

judgments.title: PEMOHON 1. ) LEE YOKE SWEE 2. ) YANG CAIJIAO
judgments.catchwords: Probate and Administration — Removal of administrator — Joint administrators — Application under section 34 Probate and Administration Act 1959 — Cross-applications for removal of co-administrators — Meaning of "sufficient cause" — Scope of Court's supervisory jurisdiction — Fiduciary duties of personal representatives — Duty to cooperate and participate in joint administration — Whether family hostility and disagreements alone sufficient to justify removal — Whether cumulative post-appointment conduct demonstrated persistent non-cooperation and failure to discharge fiduciary obligations — Whether continued joint administration had ceased to function effectively — Court's intervention directed to proper administration of estate rather than punishment of administrator — One application allowed and cross-application dismissed. Cases referred to: Re Khoo Boo Gong decd; Khoo Teng Seong v Teoh Chooi Ghim & Ors [1981] 2 MLJ 68. Tan Kah Fatt & Anor v Tan Ying [2023] 2 MLJ 583
judgments.judge: YA Puan Mahazan Binti Mat Taib

judgments.title: EURO SAGA SDN BHD v PROTOTECH SDN BHD
judgments.catchwords: Sale of goods — Ready-mix concrete — Supply of Grade C32/40T2 concrete — Defective concrete — Cube tests and coring test failed — Breach of implied conditions as to description and fitness for purpose — Sections 15 & 16(1)(a), Sale of Goods Act 1957. Contract — Breach of contract — Privity of contract — Supply agreement — Whether supplier liable for defective concrete — Rectification costs — Remoteness of damage — Mitigation of loss — Section 74, Contracts Act 1950. Evidence — Cube tests — Coring test — Non-Conformance Product Form — Admissions by supplier's witnesses — Scientific evidence — Whether sufficient to establish breach of contractual specification. Appeal — Appellate intervention — Misapprehension of evidence — Wrong application of doctrine of privity of contract — Failure to appreciate objective test results
judgments.judge: YA Tuan Shamsul Bahrin bin Abdul Manaf

judgments.title: SURIWONG INTERNATIONAL SDN. BHD. v Menteri Kewangan, Malaysia & ors

parties.label: RESPONDEN
names: Pengarah Negeri Jabatan Kastam Diraja Malaysia Di Kedah

judgments.catchwords: The legality of imposition of anti-dumping duty (“ADD”) on the importation of cellulose cement fibre products from a company in Thailand known as TPI Polene. Can anti-dumping duty be imposed on the import of a product from a foreign company based on a previous or one-off preliminary determination of dumping and injury that took place to determine to the dumping and injury caused by 3 different foreign companies under the generic term “others”. Whether the Minister in exercising his power under section 14A of the Customs Act 1967 and section 62 of the Goods and Services Tax Act 2014 to refuse remission of anti-dumping duty must give reasons for his decision

YA Dato' Seri Vazeer Alam bin Mydin Meera

judgments.title: MASTER HIGH ENGINEERING SDN.BHD. & ors v UMAR ZEN & ors
judgments.catchwords: Tort – Deceit – Fraudulent misrepresentation – Fabricated bank transfer receipt – False representation that payment made using own funds – Plaintiffs induced to pay SBLC commission and diesel venture advance – Documents marked for identification not relied upon – Material facts admitted in pleadings – Unrebutted oral evidence – First defendant’s failure to testify – Adverse inference under s 114(g) Evidence Act 1950 – Second defendant’s knowledge and participation – Joint liability – Third defendant not involved – Jurisdiction established – Consultancy claim unproved – Judgment partly allowed – Interest and costs awarded

judgments.title: ROSLI A/L JENDUT & ors v AGROBEST (M) SDN. BHD & ors
judgments.catchwords: Notice of Appeal filed by defendants against the whole of the High Court’s decision eventhough the dismissal of the plaintiffs’ claim for special and exemplary damages were in defendants favour — whether plaintiffs ought to have filed a Notice of Appeal if they had wanted to appeal against the dismissal of their claim for special and exemplary damages —-Rules 5,8, 1A, 102, 103, 104, 105 Rules of Court of Appeal 1994 (RCA 1994), s 69(5) Courts of Judicature Act 1964 (CJA) —Proof of the Indigenous People of their connection to the deceased persons’ tombstones and grave sites — use of oral tradition and narratives under s 32(1)(d) and (e) Evidence Act 1950 (EA) — locus of the plaintiffs to sue for trespass for the demolition and desecration of their ancestors’ tombstones and grave sites —- nature of customary title — whether it has been extinguished before the alienation and issuance of land titles to private corporations —- effect of a 4(2)(a) National Land Code (NLC) on the law for the time being in force on customary tenure — whether Article 5(1) on right to life covers the protection of the indigenous peoples’ burial sites, custom and tradition —United Nations Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples (UNDRIP) and its use in interpreting Constitutional Rights.
judgments.judge: YA Dato' Nordin Bin Hassan

YA Dato' Seri Vazeer Alam bin Mydin Meera

judgments.title: AMRAN BIN MUDA & ors v Kerajaan Negeri Terengganu
judgments.catchwords: CONTRACT — Formation — Oral representation — Whether oral representation gave rise to legitimate expectation for contract extension — Representation that extension "may be considered" and "subject to government approval" — Whether such representation was clear and unequivocal — Held, representation was conditional and ambiguous — No legitimate expectation arose CONTRACT — Estoppel — Promissory estoppel — Elements — Clear and unequivocal promise — Reliance — Inequity — Whether promissory estoppel could be invoked against State Government — Whether representation made without authority under s 3 Government Contracts Act 1949 could bind State Government — Whether doctrine could override statutory provisions CONTRACT — Consideration — Pre-existing contractual obligation — Whether purchase of compactor lorry constituted fresh consideration for alleged promise of extension — Contractual term required provision of lorry in second year — Whether practical benefit test from Williams v Roffey Bros accepted in Malaysia CONTRACT — Government Contracts Act 1949, s 3 — Contracts on behalf of State Government — Requirement of written authority by Menteri Besar — Whether oral representation by Principal Assistant Secretary could bind State Government — Evidence Act 1950, s 91 — Extrinsic evidence — Whether oral representation could be admitted to vary written contract — Contract provided for 3-year term without automatic extension EQUITY — Promissory estoppel — Shield and sword distinction — Whether promissory estoppel could be used as cause of action to claim damages — Held, promissory estoppel is a shield, not a sword — Cannot create enforceable contract where none existed — Cannot be used to claim damages for breach of alleged promise ADMINISTRATIVE LAW — Legitimate expectation — Whether doctrine applies in private law — Contract for waste collection services — Relationship between State Government and private contractor — Whether public law element present EVIDENCE — Proof of loss — Special damages — Financial statements without supporting account books — Whether admissible to prove loss of profits
judgments.judge: YA Tuan Yusrin Faidz bin Yusoff

judgments.title: K.A.D BINA ENTERPRISE SDN BHD v Kerajaan Negeri Terengganu
judgments.catchwords: CONTRACT — Formation — Oral representation — Whether oral representation gave rise to legitimate expectation for contract extension — Representation that extension "may be considered" and "subject to government approval" — Whether such representation was clear and unequivocal — Held, representation was conditional and ambiguous — No legitimate expectation arose CONTRACT — Estoppel — Promissory estoppel — Elements — Clear and unequivocal promise — Reliance — Inequity — Whether promissory estoppel could be invoked against State Government — Whether representation made without authority under s 3 Government Contracts Act 1949 could bind State Government — Whether doctrine could override statutory provisions CONTRACT — Consideration — Pre-existing contractual Besar — Whether oral representation by Principal Assistant Secretary could bind State Government — Evidence Act 1950, s 91 — Extrinsic evidence — Whether oral representation could be admitted to vary written contract — Contract provided for 3-year term without automatic extension EQUITY — Promissory estoppel — Shield and sword distinction — Whether promissory estoppel could be used as cause of action to claim damages — Held, promissory estoppel is a shield, not a sword — Cannot create enforceable contract where none existed — Cannot be used to claim damages for breach of alleged promise ADMINISTRATIVE LAW — Legitimate expectation — Whether doctrine applies in private law — Contract for waste collection services — Relationship between State Government and private contractor — Whether public law element present EVIDENCE — Proof of loss — Special damages — Financial statements without supporting account books — Whether admissible to prove loss of profits
judgments.judge: YA Tuan Yusrin Faidz bin Yusoff

judgments.title: YS CHONG ENTERPRISE SDN BHD v PERKASA JAUHARI SDN BHD (IN LIQUIDATION)
judgments.catchwords: Construction Industry Payment and Adjudication Act 2012 – a question of considerable Doctrinal importance at the intersection of construction law and insolvency law: what is the effect, upon a winding-up order against a company which was founded upon an adjudication decision obtained by that company’s creditor, of a subsequent final determination by the court which conclusively reverses that adjudication decision on the merits - does the winding up order — by itself, and notwithstanding that the adjudication decision upon which it was founded has been extinguished under s. 13(c) of CIPAA by the final court determination — have the effect of transmuting the adjudication decision into a binding, permanent debt which survives that final determination, and which the CIPAA claimant may thereafter invoke as a mutual set-off under s. 526 of the Companies Act 2016 (“CA 2016”) against a winding-up petition subsequently presented by the aggrieved debtor company whose claims were vindicated in those very court proceedings? - the meaning of “finally decided by arbitration or the court” in s. 13(c), whether final determination means the decision must have been “decided, and no longer open to any form of challenge or appeal.
judgments.judge: YA Dato' Ong Chee Kwan
coram: YA Datuk Seri Mohd Firuz Bin Jaffril
YA Dato' Ong Chee Kwan
YA Tuan Dean Wayne Daly

docs.name: Alasan Penghakiman
docs.preparedBy: YA Dato' Ong Chee Kwan

judgments.title: MOHD LUQMANNULHAKIM BIN RAZAK v Pendakwa Raya
judgments.catchwords: Pemohon telah dituduh di bawah seksyen 15(1)(a) Akta Dadah Berbahaya 1952 (ADB) dibaca bersama dengan seksyen 39C(1)(b) Akta yang sama – peruntukan khas di dalam ADB adalah terpakai dalam permohanan jamin - seksyen 41B(1)(b) ADB tidak membenarkan jamin – undang-undang khusus – undang-undang am- maksim generalia specialibus derogant - peruntukan khusus adalah mengatasi pemakaian peruntukan am yang terdapat di dalam Akta yang melibatkan pemakaian prosiding jenayah secara am sebagai contohnya Kanun Tatacara Jenayah (KTJ). - permohonan jamin di bawah seksyen 388 KTJ adalah tidak terpakai – Pemohon gagal membuktikan terdapat keadaan khas di dalam permohonannya – Permohonan Pemohon ditolak – Bantahan awal oleh Responden (Pendakwaan) dibenarkan
judgments.judge: YA Puan Suriyati Hasimah binti Mohd Hashim

judgments.title: Q RESOURCES (M) SDN BHD v MAGNUM CONSORTIUM SDN BHD & ors
judgments.catchwords: Fraudulent deprivation and unlawful alienation of the Plaintiff's primary corporate assets—five valuable parcels of development land - declaration that the Sale SPA dated 8.3.2010 and the transfer between the Plaintiff and the 1st Defendant are null and void ab initio - a declaration that the 1st Defendant holds the said properties as a constructive trustee for the Plaintiff - counter-claim- Plaintiff’s action against them amounted to an abuse of the court’s process - An order that the 2nd and 4th Defendants are entitled to claim from the Plaintiff contribution and/or damages and/or indemnity, for all losses incurred by the 2nd and 4th Defendants because of any legal claimed experienced by the 2nd and 4th Defendants arising from the Plaintiff's actions in filing this Civil.
judgments.judge: YA Dato' Rozana Binti Ali Yusoff

judgments.title: HENG OOI HONG v Pendakwa Raya
judgments.catchwords: STATUTORY INTERPRETATION Catchwords: Construction of statutes - Rules of statutory interpretation - Literal rule - Golden rule - Purposive approach - Interpretation Acts 1948 and 1967, Section 17A - Grammatical meaning - Conjunctive word "and" (dan) - Whether literal interpretation yields an absurd or conflicting outcome - Word "and" construed as disjunctive "or" (atau) - Separation of powers - Role of the judiciary - Strict limits on reading additional words into penal statutes - Legislative defect or lacuna - Remedy lying exclusively with the Legislature - Subsequent legislative amendment - Fisheries (Amendment) Act 2025 (Act A1780) - Effect of subsequent amendment as conclusive legislative acknowledgment of a lacuna. CRIMINAL PROCEDURE Catchwords: Revision - Revisionary jurisdiction of the High Court - Criminal Procedure Code, Section 323 - Courts of Judicature Act 1964, Section 31 - supervisory and revisionary power over criminal proceedings in subordinate courts - Scope of revision - Correction of illegal, incorrect, or improper orders - Distinction between revision and coordinate review - Plea of guilty - Voluntary and informed plea - Criminal Procedure Code, Section 173(b) - Strict pre-plea procedural safeguards - Failure of trial Judge to ensure accused fully understands nature and consequences of plea - Consequential livelihood-depriving penalties - Cancellation of commercial fishing license - Failure to state consequential penalties on the face of the charge sheet - Failure of prosecution to provide written notice - Failure of court to explain penalty prior to recording plea - Whether post-plea verbal assertions by prosecuting officer can cure a procedural defect - Forfeiture - Mandatory vs discretionary forfeiture - Forfeiture ordered under wrong statutory power - Attempt by prosecution to retrospectively substitute discretionary forfeiture under Section 34 of the Fisheries Act 1985 to cure defective mandatory forfeiture order under Section 52(1). FISHERIES Catchwords: Offences - Fisheries Act 1985, Section 8(a), Section 11(3)(a), Section 14(4)(b) - Operating vessel without valid license - Consequential sentencing orders - Forfeiture and disposal of vessel - Fisheries Act 1985, Section 52(1) - Prerequisites for mandatory vessel forfeiture - Necessity to satisfy both limb (a) and limb (b) of Section 52(1) conjunctively - Charge of operating vessel without valid license - Impossibility of violating license conditions for a non-existent license - Ultra vires order - Charge of using a license issued in the name of another person under Section 14(4)(b) - Omission of Section 14(4)(b) from the mandatory forfeiture regime of Section 52(1)(a) - Failure of subordinate court to bifurcate charges - Cancellation of fishing license - Fisheries Act 1985, Section 33(1) - Summary cancellation of license belonging to an uncharged third-party owner - Jurisdictional prerequisites of Section 33(1) - Nexus between license and physical offence. CONSTITUTIONAL LAW / NATURAL JUSTICE Catchwords: Fundamental liberties - Right to a fair procedure - Article 5(1) of the Federal Constitution - Rules of natural justice - Right to be heard (audi alteram partem) - Summary deprivation of proprietary right and primary livelihood - Cancellation of fishing license belonging to a registered third party - Third party never joined as a party to criminal proceedings or afforded opportunity to show cause - Unconstitutionality of condemning an unheard third party through the side-wind of an operator's guilty plea. WORDS & PHRASES Catchwords: "And" (dan) - "Or" (atau) - Fisheries Act 1985, Section 52(1) - Conjunctive vs disjunctive construction - "Owner" (pemunya) - Fisheries Act 1985, Section 2.
judgments.judge: YA Dr John Lee Kien How @ Mohammad Johan Lee

judgments.title: Axventure Sdn. Bhd. v Ambank (M) Berhad & ors
judgments.catchwords: CONSTRUCTION LAW — Performance Bond — Arbitration Act 2005, section 11 — Injunction in aid of arbitration restraining call on unconditional Performance Bond — Setting aside ex parte injunction for material non-disclosure — Duty of full and frank disclosure — Whether omissions concerning advance payment, moratorium, without prejudice negotiations and ex parte urgency were material — Whether beneficiary's call on Performance Bond was unconscionable — Whether disputed issues of delay, payment certification, stop work order, termination and replacement contractor, viewed cumulatively, established unconscionability — Whether contractual disputes alone satisfy the threshold for unconscionability — Balance of convenience — Whether Performance Bond should be released pending arbitration — Whether inquiry into damages under cross-undertaking should be deferred.
judgments.judge: YA Tuan Rajes Raghavji

judgments.title: RAMACHANDRAN A/L VERASAMY & ors v Pendakwa Raya
judgments.catchwords: Appeal against conviction and sentence – s 39B (1) (a) Dangerous Drugs Act 1952 – absence of s 34 of the Penal Code – omission to include in charge – defective charge - prove on higher threshold - control and knowledge – common intention - DNA found – cigarette – towel – unauthorized retention of keys – termination of tenancy – unauthorized access – accepted invitation – no duress – opportunity to decline – consensus – willing participation – bare assertion – substantial quantity of drugs – failed to rebut statutory presumption – presumption of trafficking - adverse inference – s 114 (g) Evidence Act 1950 – failure to call material witness – transfer of ownership – dealing of property – tenancy agreement - mere proximity – flagrant incompetency of counsel – failed to conduct trial competently – failed to cross-examine – miscarriage of justice – different styles in trials
judgments.judge: YAA Tun Wan Ahmad Farid Bin Wan Salleh
coram: YAA Tun Wan Ahmad Farid Bin Wan Salleh

YA Datuk Azimah binti Omar

judgments.title: SIVACHANDRAN A/L S JAYARAJAH v Pendakwa Raya
judgments.catchwords: Review jurisdiction - Rule 137 of the Rules of the Federal Court 1995 -Inherent powers of Federal Court – All sentences passed to be in accordance with the law – Avoiding miscarriage of justice - Exceptional circumstances - Fresh evidence of prisoner’s death following whipping. Constitutional Law - Federal Constitution - Articles 5(1), 8(1) and 8(2) Fundamental liberties - Life and personal liberty - Human dignity - Equal protection of the law - Whether it is in violation of medical ethics for medical officer to be participate in the execution of whipping. Judicial whipping - Corporal punishment - Whether judicial whipping amounts to cruel, inhuman and degrading treatment (“CIDT”) - Whether punishment of whipping is inconsistent with constitutional protection of life, dignity and bodily integrity. Whether whipping is disproportionate and therefore contrary to Article 8(1) - Whether exemption of women from whipping constitutes prohibited discrimination on ground only of gender is contrary to Art 8(2) - Intelligible differentia. Whether whipping is against Art. 5 of the Universal Declaration of Human Rights (“UDHR”) and the ASEAN Human Rights Declaration in its prohibitions against CIDT - Whether whipping is against customary international law.
judgments.judge: YAA Tun Wan Ahmad Farid Bin Wan Salleh
coram: YAA Tun Wan Ahmad Farid Bin Wan Salleh
YAA Datuk Hajah Azizah binti Haji Nawawi
YA Dato' Lee Swee Seng

docs.preparedBy: YAA Tun Wan Ahmad Farid Bin Wan Salleh

judgments.title: KAMALESWARAN THAVER A/L SUPPIAH v Pendakwa Raya & ors
judgments.catchwords: Prosedur Jenayah — Rayuan terhadap sabitan dan hukuman — Tertuduh didakwa mengedar dadah berbahaya di bawah s 39B(1)(a) Akta Dadah Berbahaya 1952 (ADB) - boleh dihukum di bawah s 39B (2) ADB — Sama ada pembelaan dipertimbangkan secukupnya oleh Hakim Perbicaraan — Sama ada hakim perbicaraan terkhilaf dalam keputusan – Mahkamah ini mengurangkan pertuduhan kepada pertuduhan di bawah seksyen 12(2) ADB, dan boleh dihukum di bawah s 39A(2) ADB - Tertuduh dijatuhkan hukuman pemenjaraan selama 10 tahun dari tarikh tangkapan dan 10 kali sebatan rotan. Keterangan – dalam kes menyokong tuduhan pemilikan dadah dan bukannya pengedaran dadah.
judgments.judge: YA Datuk Hayatul Akmal binti Abdul Aziz
coram: YA Dato' Azmi Bin Ariffin
YA Datuk Hayatul Akmal binti Abdul Aziz
YA Datuk Meor Hashimi bin Abdul Hamid

judgments.title: LIM CHENG LING v UDA HOLDINGS BERHAD & ors
judgments.catchwords: Moving escalator in shopping mall collapsed under Plaintiff’s feet – Plaintiff’s leg trapped inside a gap in the escalator for 45 minutes – Plaintiff suffered Crush Injury, nerve damage and sartorius muscles cut – Occupiers’ liability in Malaysia different from codified United Kingdom’s Occupiers' Liability Act 1957 – In Malaysia, occupier’s duty of care varies for different groups of visitors – Visitors to a shopping mall are licensees of the commercial building – Duty not to expose the licensees to hidden perils and to warn him of existing traps or concealed danger – Occupier owes a non-delegable duty to ensure that the duty of care was exercised by his contractor – Statutory duty under the Factories and Machinery Act 1967 – Definition of “machinery” included elevators and escalators – Certification and maintenance by the occupier are mandatory – REVISED COMPENDIUM OF PERSONAL INJURY AWARDS is merely a guideline and not binding – Aggravated Damages to be awarded only if there is evidence of aggravating circumstances – Judicial notice that a fracture of Posterior Cortex of Proximal Right Femur could cause the leg to be unstable and to suffer a permanent limp – Long lasting trauma suffered justified award for Post-Traumatic Stress Disorder – Erectile dysfunction – Award for medical expenses not limited to Allopathic medical treatment – Evidence of expenses incurred may be proven through documentary evidence or oral evidence if documentary evidence is not available – EPF forms and Form EA are contemporaneous documents for proof of loss of income
judgments.judge: YA Tuan Gan Techiong

judgments.title: Pusat Pakar Tawakal Sdn. Bhd. . v Datin Nor Rizam Bt Mohd Kenali (Menyaman Sebagai Pentadbir Estet Dato' Ir Zainudin Bin A Kadir) & ors

parties.label: RESPONDEN
names: Datin Nor Rizam Bt Mohd Kenali (Menyaman Sebagai Pentadbir Estet Dato' Ir Zainudin Bin A Kadir)
Dato' Dr Haji Mohd Solahuddin bin Mohd Kenali

judgments.catchwords: A. Medical Negligence – Standard of care In cases involving diagnosis and treatment, the applicable standard of care is the Bolam test, subject to the Bolitho qualification that the expert opinion relied upon must withstand logical analysis. B. Medical Negligence – Duty to advise The reasonable prudent patient test applies only to a doctor's duty to advise of material risks and not to diagnosis or treatment. C. Medical Negligence – Expert evidence Where expert opinions conflict, the court is entitled to prefer the opinion that is logical, supported by contemporaneous records and consistent with the evidence. D. Medical Negligence – Post-operative care Premature discharge from the recovery bay without adequate assessment of airway, ventilation and recovery from anaesthesia constituted a breach of the standard of care. E. Medical Negligence – Respiratory monitoring Failure to adequately monitor the patient's respiratory status during the post-operative period amounted to negligence. F. Medical Negligence – Airway management Delay in oxygenation and incorrect intubation resulting in hypoxia constituted a breach of the duty of care. G. Medical Negligence – Medical records Incomplete and inadequate medical records may support a finding that the standard of care was not met. H. Medical Negligence – Hospital liability A hospital owes a non-delegable duty of care to ensure competent treatment, adequate facilities and proper post-operative care, notwithstanding that the treating doctors are independent contractors. I. Medical Negligence – Causation A series of negligent acts and omissions which materially contributed to the patient's hypoxic brain injury established causation.
judgments.judge: YA Datuk Azhahari Kamal bin Ramli
coram: YA Datuk Ravinthran a/l Paramaguru
YA Datuk Azhahari Kamal bin Ramli
YA Dato' Ahmad Fairuz bin Zainol Abidin

docs.name: Alasan Penghakiman
docs.preparedBy: YA Datuk Azhahari Kamal bin Ramli

judgments.title: KUMARESAN A/L THURAIRAJU v LEE YONG WUN, PAULINE
judgments.catchwords: Civil Procedure — Appeal — Section 28(1)(b) Courts of Judicature Act 1964 — Scope of appellate jurisdiction following MT Ventures — Test for determining whether an interlocutory order constitutes an appealable "decision" — Refusal of summary judgment and judgment on admissions raising threshold question of statutory interpretation under the Legal Profession Act 1976 — Whether order conclusively determined parties' substantive rights notwithstanding interlocutory character — Appeal competent — Application to strike out appeal dismissed.
judgments.judge: YA Tuan Samry bin Masri

judgments.title: MOHD FIRDAUS BIN MOHD ZAHID v Pendakwa Raya
judgments.catchwords: Perayu dituduh pertuduhan pindaan kerusi sebelah penumpang kereta Sekatan Jalan Raya SJR mengedar berbahaya Methamphetamine berat Bersih 299.17 gram Seksyen 39B(1)(a) Akta Dadah Berbahaya 1952 Dihukum 39B(2) pembelaan membela diri hakim bicara disabitkan menjatuhkan hukuman pemenjaraan fakta pendakwaan pasukan serbuan mencurigakan melayu penumpang Surat rentas negeri bahu barang salah beg pinggang berzip paket plastic pil biji dibaluti pita pelekat penahanan jagaan kawalan pengetahuan laporan polis dianalisis ahli kimia disenaraikan Jadual Pertama prima facie kelakuan munasabah relevan berat statutory mengakui penafian rekaan kredibel dipercayai keraguan munasabah kenderaan bersetuju bersalah anggapan mematahkan mantap menilai keterangan kukuh special compartment pembawa ikhlas suci hati innocent carrier

judgments.title: MOHD SAFUAN BIN RAMLI v Pendakwa Raya & ors
judgments.catchwords: Seksyen 302 Kanun Keseksaan – Pembuktian elemen niat untuk membunuh – Pembelaan tidak waras (unsound mind) menurut Seksyen 84 Kanun Keseksaan – Pembuktian pada tahap atas imbangan kebarangkalian (on the balance of probabilities) – legal insanity dan medical insanity – Peringkat pembuktian pertama iaitu medical insanity telah gagal dilepasi oleh Tertuduh
judgments.judge: YA Dato' Paduka Azman Bin Abdullah
coram: YA Dato' Paduka Azman Bin Abdullah
YA Datuk Hayatul Akmal binti Abdul Aziz
YA Datuk Mohd Radzi Bin Abdul Hamid

docs.preparedBy: YA Dato' Paduka Azman Bin Abdullah

judgments.title: Pendakwa Raya v IPZUMA BINTI AMBO
judgments.catchwords: Warganegara Indonesia seksyen 302 304(a) Kanun Keseksaan membunuh cucu pertuduhan pilihan Homisid salah pendakwaan bersalah disabitkan hukuman pemenjaraan hakim bicara fakta kesan kecederaan badan mangsa bedah siasat laporan polis ditangkap Punca kematian perforation transverse colon blunt force trauma spina bifida congenital hydrocephalus parut tram line objek berbentuk silinder rotan penderaan koyakan usus meninggal dunia cecair rongga jangkitan tumbukan tendangan pijakan lebam punggung perut kembung peha muntah buih-buih mencubit lengan mencukupi setimpal fakta keadaan kes bersalah penghukuman asas perundangan kukuh terkhilaf hemat mempertimbangkan secukupnya prinsip penghukuman faktor-faktor pemberatan kepentingan awam pencegahan masyarakat umum memulihkan memadai serius pelanggaran kepercayaan tanggungjawab saksi mata trend

judgments.title: ABDUL RAHIM BIN WAHAP v Pendakwa Raya & ors
judgments.catchwords: Section 302 of the Penal Code – High Court sentenced the Appellant to death by hanging – The Appellant withdraw his appeal against conviction, and pursue the appeal solely against sentence – Abolition of Mandatory Death Penalty Act 2023 – Court of Appeal set aside the death sentence and substituted it with a term of thirty (30) years imprisonment and twelve (12) strokes of whipping
judgments.judge: YA Dato' Paduka Azman Bin Abdullah
coram: YA Dato' Paduka Azman Bin Abdullah
YA Dato' Azmi Bin Ariffin
YA Datuk Dr Lim Hock Leng

judgments.catchwords: Section 302 of the Penal Code - intention to murder under Section 300 of the Penal Code - Novus Actus Interveniens - Exceptions under Section 300 of the Penal Code - sudden and grave provocation - sudden fight - elements of “suddenness” and loss of self-control

judgments.title: Leong Kee Yen v Pendakwa Raya & ors
judgments.catchwords: pertuduhan mengedar dadah berbahaya Methamphetamine berat dihukum seksyen 39B(1)(a) Akta Dadah Berbahaya 1952 mesin pengimbas Perlepasan Antarabangsa KLIA bersalah disabitkan pemenjaraan seumur hidup dikecualikan hukuman sebatan 289 Kanun Tatacara Jenayah menimbangkan rayuan sebulat suara menolak rayuan mengekalkan sabitan dan hukuman pegawai kastam mencurigakan Tahanan Narkotik Balai Ketibaan Antarabangsa salotape merampas paket plastik lutsinar bahan kristal disyaki pasport borang senarai bongkar penandaan membela diri membela diri penglibatan wujud rekaan ciptaan ubat tradisional pembawa ikhlas suci hati innocent carrier munasabah telefon bimbit mesej aplikasi WeChat siasatan polis memprejudiskan diikat peha betis pemilikan jagaan kawalan pengetahuan serbuk badak upah kredibiliti disorokkan penglihatan umum mematahkan anggapan pengedaran melampaui keraguan munasabah

judgments.title: Abdulbasit Bin Baddun v Pendakwa Raya & ors
judgments.catchwords: identity and weight of the drugs- court is entitled to accept the opinion of the expert on its face value, unless it is inherently incredible- evidence in rebuttal by another expert to contradict the opinion- no evidence that the results would be affected if at all the chemicals or reagents used had passed their expiry dates- documentation recovered from the backpack as well as the conduct of the Appellant in attempting to flee and came to the conclusion that direct evidence of possession was proven
judgments.judge: YA Dato' Collin Lawrence Sequerah

YA Dato' Collin Lawrence Sequerah
YA Dato' Ahmad Kamal Bin Md. Shahid

judgments.title: Universiti Malaya (UM) v ESA JURUTERA PERUNDING SDN. BHD.
judgments.catchwords: Arbitration clause; conduct; express reservation; extension of time; Ford's Hotel Company; holistic approach; notice to produce documents referred to in pleadings; obiter dicta; other steps; Sanwell; stay of proceedings; subsection 10(1); UNCITRAL; unequivocal intention; unreported decision; waiver; Yeo Eng Lam.

judgments.title: XXXX & ors v LIM HUI JIN
judgments.catchwords: the validity of a freezing order and a seizure order of bank accounts issued by the police and Public Prosecutor (PP) respectively under the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act 2001 (AMLA); and if the above freezing order and seizure order are invalid, whether the police, PP and Government are liable for general (compensatory) damages and exemplary damages pursuant to - (a) the tort of misfeasance in public office; and/or (b) the tort of breach of statutory duty
